root_text.an4.43:1.3: Kodhagaru na saddhammagaru, makkhagaru na saddhammagaru, lābhagaru na saddhammagaru, sakkāragaru na saddhammagaru.
root_text.an4.43:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an4.43:2.1: Cattārome, bhikkhave, puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an4.43:2.3: Saddhammagaru na kodhagaru, saddhammagaru na makkhagaru, saddhammagaru na lābhagaru, saddhammagaru na sakkāragaru.
root_text.an4.43:2.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasminti.

translation_text.an4.43:1.1: “Mendicants, these four individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.43:1.3: People who value anger, or denigration, or material things, or honor rather than the true teaching.
translation_text.an4.43:1.4: These are the four individuals found in the world.
translation_text.an4.43:2.1: These four individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.43:2.3: People who value the true teaching rather than anger, or denigration, or material things, or honor.
translation_text.an4.43:2.4: These are the four individuals found in the world.
translation_text.an4.43:3.1: Mendicants who value anger and denigration,
translation_text.an4.43:3.4: that was taught by the perfected Buddha.
translation_text.an4.43:4.1: But those who value the true teaching,
translation_text.an4.43:4.2: who have lived it, and are living it now,
translation_text.an4.43:4.3: these do grow in the teaching
translation_text.an4.43:4.4: that was taught by the perfected Buddha.”
